rationale: The hip-style roofs on these Thornton, CO townhome units cast moderate-length eave shadows (estimated 8–12 ft) onto the ground, consistent with a 5:12 pitch given the fall sun angle at ~39.9°N latitude. The ridge is noticeably narrower than the building footprint but not dramatically compressed, ruling out very shallow (3:12–4:12) or very steep (8:12+) options. The triangular hip ends are moderately prominent rather than flat or sharply pointed. Regional context (Colorado suburban construction, snow-load norms) further supports a 5:12–6:12 range, and the shadow geometry favors the lower end at 5:12. Confidence is moderate due to the overhead perspective limiting precise vertical measurement.
